name: Thick and Chewy Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipeIngredient: 3/8 cup margarine (1 1/2 sticks), melted and cooled
1 cup brown sugar (light or dark), packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F.
2. Mix flour, salt, and baking soda together in a medium bowl and set aside.
3. Mix butter and sugars until well blended. Mix in egg, yolk, and vanilla extract.
4. Add dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Stir in chips and nuts to taste.
5. Form scant 1/4 cup dough into a ball. Holding the dough ball in both hands, twist apart.
6. Turn each hand up so the raw, jagged edge faces up, and put the ball back together.
7. Place on parchment-lined cookie sheet (remember the jaggy edge should face up - don't smooth the surface), about nine dough balls per sheet.
8. Bake until cookies are light golden brown and outer edges start to harden, yet centers are still soft and puffy, 15 to 18 minutes. Start checking at 13 minutes.
9. Cool cookies on cookie sheets.
Makes 18 3-inch diameter cookies.
